questions.question: What are Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) primarily designed to recognize?
questions.explanation: RNNs are specifically designed to recognize patterns in sequences such as text, genomes, and time series data.

questions.question: What learning algorithm do RNNs utilize to update their weights?
questions.explanation: RNNs use 'Backpropagation Through Time' to effectively update their weights based on calculated sequences.

questions.question: Challenges with RNNs include gradient vanishing and ___ memory across long sequences.
questions.explanation: RNNs face challenges such as gradient vanishing and exploding, which can hinder their performance on long sequences.

questions.question: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) are variants of RNNs that help improve ___ performance.
questions.explanation: LSTM and GRU are designed to enhance the performance of RNNs on sequence prediction tasks.

questions.question: In what applications are RNNs widely used?
questions.explanation: RNNs are predominantly utilized in natural language processing, time series prediction, and other sequence-related tasks.
